title: Gingered Crispy Fried Filipino Aubergine Bihon Pancit
description: Show-off Filipino dish featuring charred, bouncy-qq aubergine, double-fry-kfc style, jain-friendly, served in a dolsot stone bowl.
instructions: Heat oil in a wok or deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add sliced ginger and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add sliced aubergine and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove aubergine from oil and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together rice flour, bihon noodles, fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, water, salt, and black pepper.
Add the mixture to the oil and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is crispy and golden, about 2-3 minutes.
Return the aubergine to the oil and stir to combine with the crispy mixture.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the aubergine is well coated.
Transfer the aubergine to a dolsot stone bowl and garnish with toasted sesame seeds.
Serve immediately, with a side of steamed rice if desired.
Cook the remaining crispy mixture in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly, until it reaches a golden brown color, about 2-3 minutes.
Serve the crispy mixture on top of the aubergine, and drizzle with additional fish sauce and soy sauce if desired.
